profile.status: Open to full-time roles and freelance projects

hero.title: I build software that
hero.subtitle: survives production
hero.description: Full Stack Business Informatics graduate. I build everything from ERPs with Hexagonal Architecture to landing pages that achieve Lighthouse 100/100. I write code that other engineers can maintain without needing a translation.

sobreMi.title: Self-taught with engineering judgment, not just a code typist.
sobreMi.paragraph1: I graduated in Business Informatics from UCR in April 2025. Before that, I had already built a full ERP on my own, done an internship at Funread, and signed a contract with 4thewords. The degree was the paperwork; the projects were the real learning.
sobreMi.paragraph2: I do my best work when the problem is messy: legacy systems, migrations, race conditions, old bugs nobody wants to touch. Before writing code, I try to figure out what's actually being asked — half the solutions disappear once the problem is properly framed.
sobreMi.paragraph3_suffix: in parallel. Both formats interest me for different reasons, and they don't compete with each other.
sobreMi.footnote: // it's not a slogan. it's literally the order I work in.

projects.title: Projects that show how I think, not just what I use.
projects.description: Three project formats based on what I can share: public repositories, limited access, or NDA case studies covering the problem, technical decisions, and architecture.

First.title: New Product Development
First.body: From the initial conversation to a production-ready MVP. I choose the stack that solves the problem—not the one that's trending—and leave the codebase in a state where another engineer can pick it up without needing extra context.

Second.title: System Maintenance
Second.body: Iterating on production systems without breaking what already works. Small, reversible changes, tests where they're missing, and documentation where it's needed most.

Third.title: Bug Fixing
Third.body: Reproduce first, understand second, fix last. I enjoy tackling long-standing bugs nobody wants to touch and race conditions that only show up in production.

Fourth.title: Migrations
Fourth.body: Data, framework, or full-stack migrations. I plan short maintenance windows, preserve backward compatibility, and avoid risky big-bang releases that disrupt operations.

Header.title: How I work when you hire me for a project.
Header.description: This isn't a price list. It's how I approach each type of project. Pricing depends on the actual scope, not on a fixed rate card.

Header.title: The tools I use to solve real-world problems.
Header.description: Grouped by domain. Secondary technologies are included because I have experience with them, not because they are my go-to choice.

built: Product features in Vue.js on top of a production Python backend.
Bug fixes through systematic reproduction, not blind patches.
Small performance improvements in database queries.
4thewords.learned: Working on a live product with real users changes the definition of "done": code that works correctly but breaks operations is not finished.

built: Endpoints and models using Django + DRF.
Safe migrations with backfills split into separate steps.
funread.learned: The first time your own merge reaches production teaches more discipline than a semester of theory.

header.title: Where I got my hands on production systems.
header.description: Early in my career, but every role has a story: what I built and what I learned along the way.
